w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Jules ke paas tumhara existing frontend code hai. Hum usse ek ek page
karwayenge — naya design daalo, wiring rakho.
1. Landing page → no auth needed, simplest
3. Home / Path map → needs subjects API
5. Active quiz → needs questions API + timer
6. Lesson complete → needs XP + progress API
8. Battle lobby → needs friends + online API
Har page ke liye Jules ko kya dena hai:
Jules ko teen cheezein ek saath milti hain:
Tab hi next page pe jaana.
Jules ko yeh message do — Landing page shuru karte hain:
We are rebuilding the Gnosis frontend page by page
from scratch using a new design.
Do NOT touch any existing pages or routes yet.
Only build the Landing page at route "/".
"Learning Path | Battle Arena | Leaderboard" nav links,
XP icon + bell icon + profile avatar top right
H1: "Experience the next evolution of education"
theory with competitive arena battles to make
every lesson an epic quest for mastery."
AI mascot robot illustration (use a placeholder
div with rounded bg for now, we'll add image later)
Card 1: Gamified Learning Path icon + title + desc
Card 2: Real-time Multiplayer Battles icon + title + desc
Card 3: Global Leaderboard icon + title + desc
- This page requires NO backend connection.
- No mock data needed. Pure static page.
After building, run the dev server and confirm
Tell me what was built and show me the file structure.
Jab Jules landing page bana de aur confirm kare — mujhe batao. Main Auth page ka prompt dunga.
Ek ek page. Koi shortcuts nahi.
You said: baddiya aab dusre page k loye
baddiya aab dusre page k loye
Claude responded: Auth page ka prompt Jules ko do:
Auth page ka prompt Jules ko do:
Now build the Auth page at route "/auth".
Do NOT touch the Landing page or any other files.
DESIGN: Same warm beige/cream theme as landing page.
AI mascot robot image placeholder (rounded div)
"Namaste! I am your guide to ancient wisdom.
Let's continue your quest for knowledge."
Two tabs at top: "Log In" | "Sign Up"
Subtitle: "Access your learning path and battle arena."
Password field with lock icon + "Forgot?" link
Footer: "By continuing, you agree to our Terms of Service"
BACKEND WIRING - connect to real API:
State: { user: null, token: null, isLoading: false, error: null }
const res = await api.post('/auth/login', { email, password })
set({ user: res.data.user, token: res.data.token, isLoading: false })
set({ error: err.response?.data?.error || 'Login failed', isLoading: false })
const res = await api.post('/auth/login', { email, password })
set({ user: res.data.user, token: res.data.token, isLoading: false })
set({ error: err.response?.data?.error || 'Registration failed', isLoading: false })
- On mount: if gnosis_token exists in localStorage,
- Login form submit: call authStore.login(email, password)
- Signup form submit: call authStore.register(username, email, password)
- Show spinner on button while isLoading is true
- Show error message in red below form if error exists
- Clear error when user switches tabs
4. Create a ProtectedRoute component in src/components/ProtectedRoute.jsx:
- Checks if gnosis_token exists in localStorage
- On mount: call fetchMe() to validate token
5. Wrap these routes in App.jsx with ProtectedRoute:
- Try registering a new user → should go to /home
- Try wrong password → should show error message
- Try going to /home without login → should redirect to /auth
- Try going to /auth when already logged in → should redirect to /home
Tell me results of each test.
Jab Jules yeh complete kare aur tests pass ho jaayein — batao, Home/Path page ka prompt dunga.
Claude responded: Home / Learning Path page ka prompt Jules ko do:
Home / Learning Path page ka prompt Jules ko do:
Now build the Home page at route "/home".
Do NOT touch Landing page, Auth page, or any other files.
NAVBAR (reuse or update existing Navbar component):
- Center: "Learning Path | Battle Arena | Leaderboard" links
MAIN CONTENT - Vertical Learning Path Map:
Subtitle: "Select a subject to continue your journey."
The path is a VERTICAL SCROLLABLE list of subject nodes
connected by a vertical line — like Duolingo map.
- Circle icon on the left (connected by vertical dotted line)
COMPLETE: green checkmark circle, full progress bar
UNLOCKED/ACTIVE: orange glowing circle, partial bar,
LOCKED: grey padlock circle, greyed out text,
1. On mount, fetch two things in parallel:
For each subject, find matching progress entry.
If at least 1 level unlocked → ACTIVE
Show error message if fetch fails with retry button.
4. Clicking an ACTIVE or COMPLETE subject node:
- Path must show ALL 25 subjects — user scrolls down
- Active/current subject should have slight glow effect
- Completed subjects above, locked subjects below active
- Do 25 subjects appear in correct order?
- Is C Programming first and active/unlocked?
- Are remaining 24 subjects locked and greyed out?
- Does XP in navbar show real number?
- Does clicking C Programming go to /subject/:id?
- Does page show loading state before data arrives?
- Does page show error if backend is down?
Tell me results of each test.
Jab Jules done kare aur tests pass ho — batao, Subject Detail page next.
Claude responded: Subject Detail page ka prompt Jules ko do:
Subject Detail page ka prompt Jules ko do:
Now build the Subject Detail page at route "/subject/:subjectId".
Do NOT touch any other pages.
NAVBAR: Same as Home page (reuse Navbar component)
- Back arrow + "Back to Map" link → navigate('/home')
- Right side: robot mascot image placeholder
If LOCKED: "Complete previous to unlock" text (grey)
Days of week (M T W T F S S) — filled = active day
4. For each level, merge level data with progress status:
Map weekActivity to M T W T F S S circles
Filled orange = true, empty grey = false
LOCKED level: no action, show tooltip
- Show error with retry if API fails
- Does subject name and description show correctly?
- Do 4 levels appear with correct topics?
- Does clicking Level 1 Continue → go to /lesson/:levelId?
- Does Back to Map → go to /home?
- Does progress bar show 0% for fresh user?
Tell me results of each test.
Jab Jules done kare — batao, Active Quiz page next. Woh sabse complex page hai.
Claude responded: Active Quiz page ka prompt Jules ko do:
Active Quiz page ka prompt Jules ko do:
Now build the Active Quiz page at route "/lesson/:levelId".
This is the most complex page. Do NOT touch any other pages.
TOP BAR (no main navbar here — fullscreen quiz mode):
- Left: X button → show confirm dialog "Quit quiz?
- Center: Progress bar (orange, fills as questions complete)
- Right: XP earned so far this quiz session
- For regular questions: hint text below
Your choice will be finalized upon click."
"Select ALL correct answers, then click Lock Answer."
- Each card: letter badge (A/B/C/D) + option text
- Default state: white card with border
- Selected state (before lock): orange border highlight
Wrong selected option: red background + X mark
- Before lock: options toggle on/off on click
- Orange bar shrinking left to right
- When timer hits 0: auto-submit with no answer (0 XP)
FEEDBACK OVERLAY (shows for 1.5 seconds after answer):
setAnswers([]) // store all answers for review page
useEffect: start countdown when question changes
Clear interval on cleanup and on answer lock
Show feedback overlay with res.data (correct, xpEarned, explanation)
Store answer for review: { question, selectedOptions, correct, correctOptions, explanation }
After 1.5 seconds: move to next question
Same flow but selectedOptions is array of all selected
subjectId, // get from question or store
Do NOT save progress or award XP
- Once option is clicked/locked: cannot change answer
- Timer runs server-independently on frontend (display only)
- No submit button for single correct — click = instant lock
- Multi_correct needs explicit Lock Answer button
(varies per question — do not hardcode 10 or 20)
- Show loading spinner while questions are fetching
- If questions fetch fails: show error with "Try Again" button
- Do 10 real questions load from API?
- Does clicking an option lock it immediately?
- Does correct answer show green feedback?
- Does wrong answer show red + correct answer?
- Does XP accumulate in top right?
- Does multi_correct require Lock Answer button?
Tell me results of each test.
Yeh sabse complex page hai — Jules ko thoda time lagega. Jab done ho aur tests pass ho — batao. Lesson Complete aur Quiz Review dono ek saath next dunga kyunki woh related hain.
You said: thik h, aur ek aur bat bta de, total 16 screens h to sbke dega n.
thik h, aur ek aur bat bta de, total 16 screens h to sbke dega n. baki chal next ye 2 jo de rha h sath me de de
Claude responded: Haan — sab 16 screens ke prompts dunga, ek ek karke jaise complete hote jayenge.
Haan — sab 16 screens ke prompts dunga, ek ek karke jaise complete hote jayenge.
Abhi Lesson Complete + Quiz Review dono ek saath:
Now build TWO pages together since they are closely related:
Do NOT touch any other pages.
- No main navbar — standalone completion screen
- Robot mascot image with "EXCELLENT!" banner badge
- Subtitle: "You've unlocked the mysteries of [level topic] module."
Motivational text: "You're on fire! Keep the flame alive."
Week days M T W T F S S (filled = active)
Arrow button to start next level
1. Read data from location.state (passed from quiz page):
2. On mount — do these API calls ONCE:
Use a ref to prevent duplicate calls:
Show these three lines in breakdown card.
If nextLevel exists: show next level info
If nextSubject exists: show "New Subject Unlocked!"
- Back arrow + "Back to Results" → navigate back
- "Faster than X%" badge (just show motivational text)
For each of the 10 questions show a card:
- User's answer highlighted green with checkmark
- User's wrong answer highlighted red with X
Pagination if needed (1 2 3 pages of questions)
"Start Next Module →" button → navigate to next level
Show subject name and level number
all answer data comes from sessionStorage
Read nextLevelId from sessionStorage if stored,
- Complete a quiz and reach this page
- Does XP breakdown show correct numbers?
- Is next level card showing Level 2?
- Does "Review Answers" go to review page?
- Does direct URL access redirect to /home?
- Is level marked complete in DB after visiting?
- Are correct ones green with checkmark?
- Are wrong ones red showing correct answer?
- After navigating away, is sessionStorage cleared?
Tell me results of all tests.

Yeh 2 pages complete hone ke baad — Battle section shuru hoga. Woh 6 pages hain (lobby, challenge sent, active battle, results, host create, host lobby, participant waiting). Thoda complex hai socket wiring ke saath.
You said: ha battle section ka de.

ha battle section ka de. bari bari -ye imp part h jisme 1-1 jaise rela itme pe sb chal rha h , notification v , sent reject timer, question , etc bhut kuch h. to thoda dhyan me rkhte hu, aesa dena n ki jisse jules review v kr le ek bar ki haa ye logic implmented h sbka, aur shai wired h baki de apna jo de rha tha
Claude responded: Battle section 6 pages hain.
Step 1 — Jules ko pehle yeh do (socket setup, koi page nahi):
Before building any battle pages, set up the
Socket.io foundation. Do NOT build any pages yet.
In login action: after setting user, call socket.connect()
In logout action: call socket.disconnect() before clearing state
Tell me: is socket connecting successfully?
Jab socket connected confirm ho — yeh do (Battle Lobby):
Now build the Battle Lobby page at route "/battle".
Do NOT touch any other pages.
Challenge friends or lead a legion."
Right side: AI mascot robot image
Challenge a friend to get started!"
// Check online status for all friends
containing search input (client-side, no API call)
User picks subject → then picks level (1-4)
Confirm button → emit socket event + navigate
Auto-dismiss after 30 seconds if no action
6. Use useSocketConnect() hook at top of component.
- Does friend list show with real friends from API?
- Are online friends showing green dot?
- Does clicking Challenge open subject selector?
- Does Join with Code input + button work?
- Open two browser windows, login different users
Does challenge popup appear on receiver's screen?
Jab yeh done ho — Challenge Sent + Active Battle + Results teen pages ek saath dunga. Woh tightly connected hain socket events se.
Claude responded: Challenge Sent + Active Battle + Battle Results — teeno ek saath:
Challenge Sent + Active Battle + Battle Results — teeno ek saath:
Now build THREE battle pages together. They are
Do NOT touch any other pages.
Below circle: opponent avatar with their initial
// After 2 seconds go back to lobby
4. Safety: if location.state is null navigate('/battle')
This page is used for BOTH 1v1 and group quiz.
Detect mode from location.state.isBattle or roomId.
- Left: Your avatar + username + your score (blue)
(For group: show "X Players" instead of one opponent)
- Same as lesson quiz but different styling
- Default: white card with subtle border
- Option letter badge: A B C D
For multi_correct: "Lock Answer" button below options
- Shrinks orange arc as time passes
// For group quiz joining (if came from /battle/join)
setFeedback({ correct: false, xpEarned: 0, rejected: true })
Server emits quiz:question again for next
Remove all socket listeners listed above
- "Champion of the Arena" subtitle for victory
- Their score (large, red if lost, green if won)
Note: "Room XP only — does not affect global rank"
(Find current user in results.top3 or results.allPlayers)
Battle room XP is already calculated by server.
Do NOT call /xp/award here — battle XP
is room-scoped only, handled by battle-service.
Do NOT update global leaderboard from this page.
Before saying done, verify these socket flows:
□ challenge:rejected shows rejection message then redirects
□ Answer click emits quiz:answer with correct format
□ quiz:results navigates to results page with data
□ location.state null checks on all 3 pages
□ No duplicate socket listeners (off before on)
□ Timer stops when answer is locked
□ Timer auto-submits at 0 with empty array
2. Any socket events that could not be tested
3. Any errors in console during build
Yeh teen pages complete hone ke baad — Group Quiz pages (Host Create + Host Lobby + Participant Waiting) dunga. Woh last battle pages hain.
You said: thik h de de,
Claude responded: Group Quiz — teeno pages ek saath:
Group Quiz — teeno pages ek saath:
Now build THREE group quiz pages together.
They share socket events and are closely connected.
Do NOT touch any other pages.
Pill buttons: Easy | Medium | Hard | Mixed
Disabled until at least 1 question saved
On click: emit socket + navigate to host lobby
Multiple can be marked correct (multi_correct support)
Number + first 50 chars of question text
Click to load back into editor
questions = [] // array of saved question objects
Validate: at least one option not empty
Validate: at least one correct option marked
Reset editor to blank new question
TOP: "Host Mode" badge in navbar area
- Copy icon button next to code
- "X Questions • Xs per answer • Speed Bonus Active"
// Host joins their own room socket
Case A: User came from Join with Code button
→ roomId in URL is the code they entered
Random fact from array of 5 hardcoded facts
- "The session will begin as soon as host is ready."
"Almost there! Waiting for a few more."
LAYOUT CASE B (no room code / direct access):
// Show error: "Room not found" or "Quiz already started"
Show error message + "Back to Lobby" button
JULES REVIEW CHECKLIST — verify before done:
□ Multiple correct options can be marked
□ Saved questions list updates after save
□ Edit saved question loads it back to editor
□ Delete saved question removes from list
□ Generate Code disabled until 1+ questions saved
□ Room code displayed correctly from URL/state
□ Player count badge updates in real-time
□ Host is NOT in the participants list as a player
□ Code entry form shows if no roomCode in URL
□ socket.emit('room:join') fires on mount with roomCode
□ Host shown with HOST badge in participant list
□ Current user shown as "You" in participant list
□ Host sees participant appear in grid
3. Cross-page flow test results if you can test it
Yeh teen pages ke baad sirf
— Leaderboard aur Profile. Woh simple hain, socket nahi hai, sirf API calls.
You said: thik h de de
Claude responded: Leaderboard + Profile — dono ek saath:
Now build the final TWO pages:
Do NOT touch any other pages.
These are the last two pages. No socket needed.
- Trophy icon + "Hall of Fame" title (large, centered)
- Subtitle: "See how you stack up against
Master one more lesson to climb the ranks!"
Orange filled = active tab, outlined = inactive
1st place (center, tallest, gold border + crown icon):
Columns: Rank | Avatar + Username | Total XP
"League resets in X days X hours"
"Go to Battle Arena to challenge friends!" CTA
1. On mount, fetch based on active tab:
2. On tab switch: fetch that tab's data
Cache results — don't re-fetch if already loaded
Global: use currentUserRank from API response
If rank > 20: add extra row at bottom
showing current user with their actual rank
Calculate ms until next Monday 00:00 UTC:
5. Podium: take first 3 from leaderboard array
Large avatar circle (first 2 letters of username)
Settings gear icon (top right of card)
Card 1: Lightning icon | Total XP | "TOTAL XP"
Card 2: Fire icon | X Days | "CURRENT STREAK"
Earned = first 3 if user has any battles/progress
List of subjects user has started:
"Challenge a peer to an Arena Battle"
Show "Top X%" (assume 1000 total users for now)
{ name: 'First Blood', desc: 'Win your first battle',
earned: false }, // needs perfect score tracking
{ name: 'War Hero', desc: 'Win 50 battles', earned: false },
(they can find and challenge from lobby)
□ Global tab loads real data from API
□ Current user shown even if rank > 20
□ Tab switching works and caches data
□ Active subjects shown in Learning Momentum
□ Own profile shows "Ready to Duel?" card
□ Other user profile hides that card
□ All protected routes redirect to /auth if no token
□ Leaderboard shows XP earned from quiz
4. Any pages that need additional fixes
Yeh last 2 pages hain. Jab Jules complete kare — hum
Poore 16 pages ho jayenge. Ek ek page properly wired.
